stories.headline: 78th anniversary of the Nakba marked with remembrance of Palestinian displacement

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: Happy Nakba Day to all who celebrate the Arabs self-inflicted catastrophic loss in the war they started in 1948, followed by 78 years of nonstop humiliating defeats dressed up as sacred victimhood ever since 🇮🇱❤️

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: Today marks the 78th anniversary of the Nakba (catastrophe), during which more than 750,000 Palestinians were expelled and over 400 villages were destroyed to establish the State of Israel.

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.body: The #Nakba was never a moment frozen in history—it is a continuing reality of killing, displacement, siege, occupation, & exile. But memory does not die. Keys are still kept. Names are still remembered. And a people who carry their homeland in their hearts will never disappear.

stories.headline: Supreme Court rejects Virginia's bid to restore Democrat-favoring congressional map

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: Virginia Democrats try to pull a fast one and SCOTUS says, “Uh uh, grifters, not so fast.” Life is good.

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: BREAKING: The U.S. Supreme Court has denied Virginia's attempt to get its state supreme court's decision tossing out controversial election map overturned. The state's Democratic leaders had redrawn congressional maps, giving their party 10 out of 11 seats.

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: Today is Endangered Species Day. The Trump administration has rolled back protections for: —Wolverines —Sea turtles —Monarch butterflies —Bison —Polar bears —Rice's whales —Alligator snapping turtles —Saltmarsh sparrows —Florida panthers

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.body: Outright lies. Trump's assets aren't in a blind trust, and he bought and sold individual Nvidia stock in 15 separate transactions totaling millions of dollars. That's what Trump's financial disclosure - which has his signature - says.

stories.headline: Turkish company Kontrolmatik defaults on two lira bonds amid rare corporate debt default.
stories.body: Turkey’s Kontrolmatik defaulted on two separate lira bonds that matured on Friday, according to a statement by Turkey’s Central Securities Depository, marking a rare corporate debt default in the country’s local bond market

stories.headline: Marc Benioff recaps podcast discussion on AI agents and enterprise future with David Sacks and others.
stories.body: There is no substitute for bestie @DavidSacks … showed up black-shirted, caffeinated, & fully armed with enough crazy. Besties talked future of enterprise, AI agents that actually work (not just hype), and @chamath has enough truth China bombs to trigger all the Besties. Zero filter. Brilliant El Niño insight from @friedberg. I wasn’t ready for @Jason and neither was the group chat. Buckle up. This one’s XX spicy. 🌶️ 🚀 Check out the full episode here →

stories.headline: Reflection on the essential role of true friends in life.
stories.body: The most important thing in life is having true friends.

stories.headline: U.S. Attorney announces crackdown on parents of D.C. teen takeover participants
stories.body:
BREAKING: U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro announces a major crackdown on parents who let their children take part in teen takeovers that have been causing chaos throughout Washington, D.C.

Pirro vows to prosecute parents who fail to supervise their children, threatening the adults with fines and even jail times.

"If the evidence shows the parent knew or should have known or permitted or failed to prevent participation, we're going to charge them."

"If you drop your kid off and you fail to supervise them, or you let them skip school to join the chaos, you are going to face fines, court ordered classes, and possible jail time"

stories.headline: New Ebola outbreak confirmed in Congo’s eastern Ituri province with 65 deaths
stories.body: BREAKING: A new outbreak of the highly contagious Ebola virus has been confirmed in Congo’s eastern Ituri province, Africa’s top public health body said. There have been 246 suspected cases and 65 deaths recorded so far.

stories.headline: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang samples Beijing noodles during China visit
stories.body: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang was spotted trying some of Beijing’s delicacies. He was seen at No. 69 Fangzhuanchang Noodles, tucking into a bowl of “zhajiangmian” — a Beijing specialty that features noodles covered in a thick soybean-paste sauce mixed with vegetables and meat.

stories.headline: Teenage Buddhist lama blesses crowds after moving from Minneapolis suburb
stories.body: The teenage Buddhist lama, Jalue Dorje, blesses thousands at a monastery in the Himalayan foothills. Just six months earlier and thousands of miles away, he was pulling all-nighters to play Madden NFL on his Xbox at his home in a Minneapolis suburb.
